mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, invalidating the recessionary signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects tightening supply-side capacity and rising corporate output, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, with mean forward returns statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above target forces markets to price in prolonged monetary tightening and reduced consumer purchasing power, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm robust labor demand, supporting wage growth and consumer spending that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a negative or zero mean return in triggered periods over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns as investors adjust portfolio allocations.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical correlation with 5-day forward returns, or produces negative returns in > 55% of observations across all regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction activity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as recession probabilities rise.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances following a >5% housing starts decline, invalidating the recessionary signal.
